From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1937 science fiction short story by Arthur C. Clarke

"**Travel by Wire!**" is a [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") short story by English writer [Arthur C. Clarke](/wiki/Arthur_C._Clarke "Arthur C. Clarke"). His first published story, it was first published in December 1937 in the magazine *[Amateur Science Stories](/wiki/Amateur_Science_Stories "Amateur Science Stories")*. It was subsequently published as part of the collection *The Best of Arthur C. Clarke 1937-1955*. This story is a humorous record on the development of the "radio-transporter" (actually a [teleportation](/wiki/Teleportation "Teleportation") machine), and the various technical difficulties and commercial ventures that resulted.
In *[The Collected Stories of Arthur C. Clarke](/wiki/The_Collected_Stories_of_Arthur_C._Clarke "The Collected Stories of Arthur C. Clarke")*, the author calls the story (as well as his other early writings) "a kind of absolute zero from which my later writing may be calibrated".
